dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8502 IH: Heirs’ Agricultural Resources, Viability, Education, and Succession Transfer
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Figures (for himself, Mr. Thompson of Mississippi, Ms. Sewell, and Mrs. Beatty) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Agriculture

form.official-title: To direct the Secretary of Agriculture to conduct a study on ways to increase opportunities for 1890 Institutions to conduct educational programs and provide technical assistance with respect to issues relating to the transfer of agricultural land and assets, including heirs property, to the next generation of farmers and ranchers, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Heirs’ Agricultural Resources, Viability, Education, and Succession Transfer or the HARVEST Act.

section.header: Study on technical assistance with respect to transfer of agricultural land and assets

subsection.text: Not later than September 30, 2026, the Secretary of Agriculture shall conduct a study on, and submit to Congress a report on, ways to increase opportunities for 1890 Institutions (as defined in section 2 of the Agricultural Research, Extension, and Education Reform Act of 1998 (7 U.S.C. 7601)) to conduct educational programs and provide technical assistance with respect to issues relating to the transfer of agricultural land and assets, including heirs property, to the next generation of farmers and ranchers.
In this section, the term heirs property means real property held in tenancy in common which, as of the date on which a partition action is filed, satisfies all of the following requirements:

paragraph.text: There is no recorded agreement binding all the co-tenants which governs the partition of the property.
One or more of the co-tenants acquired title from a relative, whether living or deceased.

subparagraph.text: 20 percent or more of the interests are held by co-tenants who are relatives.
20 percent or more of the interests are held by an individual who acquired title from a relative, whether living or deceased.
20 percent or more of the co-tenants are relatives.
